target.title: fix(cron): add Authorization header to provider preflight probe
target.body:
## Summary

The cron job preflight check sends `GET {baseUrl}/models` to verify local provider connectivity before running a job, but was doing so **without an Authorization header**. For providers behind auth middleware (LiteLLM, vLLM, etc.), this generates spurious 401 errors on every cron run.

## Root Cause

`model-preflight.runtime.ts` -> `probeLocalProviderEndpoint()` called `fetchWithSsrFGuard` with `init: { method: "GET" }` - no auth header.

## Fix

Added `resolveProbeApiKey()` with a three-source fallback chain (config.apiKey -> auth-profiles.json -> env var). Modified `probeLocalProviderEndpoint()` to accept optional apiKey and send Bearer header. Wired apiKey resolution into `preflightCronModelProvider()`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron job preflight GET /models to local LiteLLM proxy returns 401 because no Authorization header is sent, causing "No api key passed in" errors on every cron run. Normal inference calls (POST /chat/completions) succeed because they carry the Authorization header — only the preflight probe was missing it.

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04 (Oracle VPS)
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.15.0
- **OpenClaw version:** 2026.5.18 (npm install)
- **Provider:** LiteLLM proxy at http://127.0.0.1:4000
- **Auth method:** LITELLM_API_KEY environment variable

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
# 1. Apply the patch to the running OpenClaw installation
cp dist/model-preflight.runtime-*.js /path/to/openclaw/dist/
# 2. Wait for the next scheduled cron job to trigger the preflight probe
# 3. Monitor LiteLLM proxy logs
pm2 logs litellm-proxy
# 4. Monitor OpenClaw gateway logs for cron job execution
pm2 logs openclaw-gateway
```

**Evidence after fix:** LiteLLM proxy logs before (top) and after (bottom) the patch, captured via terminal:

![Before/after terminal comparison](https://github.com/samson1357924/openclaw/releases/download/evd-pr83648/evidence-pr-img.jpg)

Before — no auth header, cron preflight triggers 401:
```text
17:30:05 LiteLLM Proxy:ERROR - Exception occured - No api key passed in.
Requester IP Address:127.0.0.1
127.0.0.1:58244 - "GET /models HTTP/1.1" 401 Unauthorized
127.0.0.1:50768 - "POST /chat/completions HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
```

After — auth header sent, preflight succeeds:
```text
127.0.0.1:45914 - "GET /models HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
127.0.0.1:49934 - "POST /chat/completions HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
```

**Observed result after fix:** GET /models response changed from 401 Unauthorized to 200 OK. No more "No api key passed in" errors in LiteLLM proxy logs. Cron preflight cache populates correctly. Normal inference calls continue to work as before — unchanged.

**What was not tested:**
- Non-default cron agent directories
- SecretRef-based auth
- Authless providers like Ollama (should be unaffected)
- OAuth/request-header auth profiles

## Changes

2 files changed:
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.ts` - auth resolution for preflight probe
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts` - 13 tests (auth coverage expanded from 4)

Closes #83648
target.author: samson1357924
